summary: Add the M4 AI persistence and agent-loop safety foundation: bounded project conversation serialization with secret redaction and an explicit opt-in round cap defaulting to ten.

decisions.decision: Persist only a strict bounded v1 conversation record containing messages and review proposals; provider secrets are redacted before serialization and active requests are never persisted.
decisions.rationale: FR-10.6 requires project-scoped durable conversations without transmitting or storing the provider key, while bounded records protect profile storage from unbounded transcript growth.

decisions.decision: Agent mode is disabled by default and, when explicitly enabled, clamps the configurable round cap to 1..100 with a default of 10 and a terminal capped state.
decisions.rationale: FR-10.5 requires explicit opt-in and a hard configurable cap; a terminal state prevents a looping mock or provider from continuing silently.

testEvidence.criterion: Persistence round-trips review state, redacts the provider secret, rejects malformed/oversized records, and the opted-in loop halts at its cap.
testEvidence.test: Run the focused persistence and loop suite once after implementation.

red.outcome: RED: the first persistence implementation inferred string roles/statuses; TypeScript reported TS2322 in conversation-persistence.ts and exited 2.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 3 passed (3); Duration 517ms.

testEvidence.criterion: The complete TypeScript and source-policy gates remain clean after the bounded type fix.
testEvidence.test: Run TypeScript and repository lint/source policy once after implementation.

red.outcome: RED: TS2322 diagnostics reported inferred role/status strings were not assignable to ConversationMessage/ProposedEdit contracts.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false; pnpm.cmd lint
green.outcome: GREEN: TypeScript exited 0; Checked 422 files in 246ms with source policy passed.

nearMisses.sought: A ready-made conversation persistence or agent-loop implementation
nearMisses.confirmation: No external source was consulted; the application-owned contract was authored from the v0.6 requirements and existing local persistence patterns.

questions: Project identity wiring, delete-conversation UI, context toggles, and actual provider-driven tool rounds remain to be integrated in later C10 units; this foundation does not claim AC-10.c/d complete.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
